verdict.answer_first: A leads with bottom line: "The critical problem: a live secret key is hardcoded in source." B starts with meta: "No problem — the review above already covers both cases."
verdict.decisiveness: A commits to one priority: "Everything else is secondary." B lists 4 actions and ends with "once you confirm," weakening the prescription.
verdict.fabrication: A invents process context not in prompt: "adversarial-review chain" and "multi-team pass"; B invents prior context with "review above" and adds unasked "handle fetch errors / non-2xx."
verdict.actionability: A is easier to execute because it gives exact first action and why: "rotate/revoke it now" and "removing the line doesn't undo the exposure, since it stays in git history."
